title: trichocline polymorpha
intro: Trichocline polymorpha, also known as New Zealand daisy or ragwort, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Trichocline polymorpha include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, colds, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Trichocline polymorpha is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal use, and it is also used in some herbal preparations to treat various health issues.
